author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

title: Chapter 92

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "Where is the saying of the Lord Christ, ‘Whosoever shall smite thee on the right cheek, turn to him the other also’?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Where is the patience which He displayed when they spat upon His face, who Himself with His most holy spittle opened the eyes of the blind?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Where is the saying of the Apostle Paul, ‘If a man smite you in the face?’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Where is that other saying of the same apostle, ‘In stripes above measure, in prisons more frequent, in deaths oft’?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: He makes mention of the sufferings which he underwent, not of the deeds which he performed.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: It had been enough for the Christian faith that these things should be done by the Jews: why do you, wretched men, do these others in addition?"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: Is it then really so, that when men smite you on the one cheek, you turn to them the other?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: This is not the report that your furious bands won for you by wandering everywhere throughout the whole of Africa with dreadful wickedness.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: I would fain have it that men should make a bargain with you, that, in accordance with the old law, you should seek but "an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth," instead of bringing out cudgels in return for the words which greet your ears.
